Launch and drive real cloud browsers on: Launch and drive real cloud browsers on TestMu AI (formerly LambdaTest) infrastructure — navigate, snapshot, click, type, read, and screenshot pages. Built for AI agents.
The cloud region that runs your browser: The cloud region that runs your browser sessions.
\nTo connect TestMu AI (formerly LambdaT:
To connect TestMu AI (formerly LambdaTest) Browser Cloud:

1. Sign in at https://accounts.lambdatest.com and open your account settings.
2. Copy your **Username** and **Access Key** (Automation credentials).
3. Choose the **Region** that matches where you want browsers to run. A session
   is bound to the region that created it, so keep this consistent across a flow.
Start a new cloud browser session and re: Start a new cloud browser session and return a Session ID used by every other action.
Open a URL in the session and return a s: Open a URL in the session and return a snapshot of the page's interactive elements.
Re-scan the current page and return its: Re-scan the current page and return its interactive elements, each with a numeric ref.
Click an element by its numeric ref from: Click an element by its numeric ref from the latest snapshot. Returns a fresh snapshot.
Type text into an element by its numeric: Type text into an element by its numeric ref. Optionally press Enter. Returns a fresh snapshot.
Read visible text from a single element: Read visible text from a single element (by ref) or the whole page body.
Capture a screenshot of the current page: Capture a screenshot of the current page as a PNG file. Returns a file reference.
Run JavaScript inside the current page a: Run JavaScript inside the current page and return its result.
End a browser session and free its resou: End a browser session and free its resources. Always run this when finished.
A label to identify this session in the: A label to identify this session in the TestMu AI dashboard.
e.g. \"latest\", \"latest-1\", or a spec: e.g. "latest", "latest-1", or a specific version number.
The operating system to run the browser: The operating system to run the browser on.
The Session ID returned by Create Sessio: The Session ID returned by Create Session.
The URL to open, including the scheme (e: The URL to open, including the scheme (e.g. https://example.com).
The numeric ref of the target element, f: The numeric ref of the target element, from the latest snapshot.
The numeric ref of the target input elem: The numeric ref of the target input element, from the latest snapshot.
The text to type into the element.
Press Enter after typing (e.g. to submit: Press Enter after typing (e.g. to submit a search or form).
The numeric ref of a specific element (f: The numeric ref of a specific element (from the latest snapshot). Leave empty to read the whole page body.
Maximum number of characters of text to: Maximum number of characters of text to return.
JavaScript to execute in the page. Use `: JavaScript to execute in the page. Use `return` to return a value; access Arguments as arguments[0], arguments[1], ...
Optional values passed to the script as: Optional values passed to the script as arguments[0], arguments[1], ... All arguments are passed as strings; parse them inside the script if you need numbers or booleans.
